categoryName: Drugs to Treat Hepatitis B and C

questions.topicName: Drugs to Treat Hepatitis B and C
questions.stem: This diagram maps the cytoplasmic replication cycle of the virus targeted by direct-acting antivirals (protease, polymerase, and NS5A inhibitors) that achieve cure rates above 95%. What type of genome does this virus carry?

explanation.summary: The figure depicts the hepatitis C virus (HCV) life cycle. HCV is a flavivirus with a positive-sense single-stranded RNA genome that can be directly translated by host ribosomes, and its NS3/4A, NS5A, and NS5B proteins are the targets of direct-acting antivirals.
explanation.whyCorrect: The figure depicts the hepatitis C virus (HCV) life cycle. HCV is a flavivirus with a positive-sense single-stranded RNA genome that can be directly translated by host ribosomes, and its NS3/4A, NS5A, and NS5B proteins are the targets of direct-acting antivirals.
